documentation.readme: o365spray is a username enumeration and password spraying tool aimed at Microsoft Office 365 (O365). This tool reimplements a collection of enumeration and spray techniques researched and identified by those mentioned in Acknowledgments.

arguments.name: --adfs-url
arguments.description: AuthURL of the target domain's ADFS login page for password

arguments.name: --enum-module
arguments.description: Specify which enumeration module to run.

arguments.name: -h
arguments.description: show this help message and exit

arguments.name: --help
arguments.description: show this help message and exit

arguments.name: --jitter
arguments.description: Jitter extends --sleep period by percentage given (0-100).

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output directory for results and test case files.

arguments.name: --paired
arguments.description: File containing list of credentials in username:password

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP/S proxy to pass traffic through

arguments.name: --safe
arguments.description: Terminate password spraying run if `N` locked accounts

arguments.name: --sleep
arguments.description: Throttle HTTP requests every `N` seconds. This can be randomized

arguments.name: --spray-module
arguments.description: Specify which password spraying module to run.

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: HTTP request timeout in seconds. Default: 25

arguments.name: --useragents
arguments.description: File containing list of user agents for randomization.

arguments.name: --validate-module
arguments.description: Specify which valiadtion module to run.

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

arguments.name: --capture
arguments.description: Capture mode: write packets to a pcap file.

examples: o365spray | Microsoft O365 User Enumerator and Password=$KFIOSA_SECRET -- v3.0.4
